text: Don't test with `sudo cat` — root can read anything and will mislead you. Test as the app's user: `sudo -u billing cat /etc/billing/billing.conf`.
`ls -l /etc/billing/billing.conf` shows `-rw------- root root` (mode 600). Only root can read it. The `billing` user gets nothing.

Grant read access to the app's user without making a secret world-readable. Cleanest: give the file to that user — `sudo chown billing /etc/billing/billing.conf` (it stays 600, now readable by `billing`). Or `sudo chgrp billing` + `sudo chmod 640`.
